manual_entries.title: Zone Minimum Outdoor Air and Minimum Airflow Setpoints (ASHRAE 62.1)
manual_entries.summary: Calculates Vmin and Vmin* based on ventilation requirements, with modifications for occupancy, windows, and CO2 sensors.

structured_payload.title: Zone Minimum Outdoor Air and Minimum Airflow Setpoints (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload.equipment_scope: VAV terminal units, fan-powered terminal units, SZVAV AHUs
structured_payload.summary: Calculates Vmin and Vmin* based on ventilation requirements, with modifications for occupancy, windows, and CO2 sensors.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone requires mechanical ventilation and project complies with ASHRAE Standard 62.1.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Calculate Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez. Set Vmin = Voz if AHU has been 100% OA for 10 min, else 1.5*Voz if 'AUTO', else as entered. Set Vmin* = Vmin except as modified by occupancy/window/CO2 conditions. For CO2 DCV, use P-only loop to reset Vmin* and Vbz-P* in parallel.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Vmin shall be equal to Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler has been supplying 100% outdoor air (outdoor air damper fully open; return air damper fully closed) for 10 minutes;

manual_entries.title: CO2 Sensor Calibration Alarm for TAV Zones
manual_entries.summary: Generates Level 3 alarm if CO2 concentration is too low (sensor may be out of calibration) or too high (exceeds setpoint).

structured_payload.title: CO2 Sensor Calibration Alarm for TAV Zones
structured_payload.summary: Generates Level 3 alarm if CO2 concentration is too low (sensor may be out of calibration) or too high (exceeds setpoint).
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone has CO2 sensor and is in TAV mode.
structured_payload.required_behavior: If CO2 < 300 ppm, or zone unoccupied >2 hours and CO2 > 600 ppm, generate Level 3 alarm indicating sensor may be out of calibration. If CO2 exceeds setpoint +10% for >10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload.fault_condition: CO2 < 300 ppm OR (unoccupied >2 hours AND CO2 > 600 ppm) OR (CO2 > setpoint +10% for >10 min)
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm.

manual_entries.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation (TAV) for Zones
manual_entries.summary: Pulse width modulates airflow setpoint when active setpoint is below controllable minimum to meet average ventilation requirements.

structured_payload.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation (TAV) for Zones
structured_payload.summary: Pulse width modulates airflow setpoint when active setpoint is below controllable minimum to meet average ventilation requirements.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and less than lowest controllable airflow Vm.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Calculate TAVratio = Vspt/Vm. Set total cycle time TCT = 15 min (adjustable). Open period OP = max(1.5 min, TCT * TAVratio). Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During open, set Vspt* = Vm; during closed, set Vspt* = 0 and disable damper loop. Randomize initial open period using RNDM*OP.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows:

chunk.cleaned_text:
64 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.2.2.  Time -Averaged Ventilation
ASHRAE Standard 62.1 and  California  Title 24 allow for ventilation to be provided based on average conditions
over a specific period of time. This  time-averaging method allows for zone airflows to effectively be controlled to
values below the VAV box controllable minimum value, which may reduce energy use and the risk of overcooling
when the zone ventilation requirement is less than the VAV box cont rollable minimum.
5.2.2.1.  When the active airflow setpoint  Vspt is nonzero  and is less than the lowest possible airflow
setpoint  allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint  shall be pulse width modulated as
follows:
a. The time-averaged ventilation ( TAV ) ratio shall be determined as TAVratio = Vspt/Vm
b. The total cycle time (TCT) shall be 15 minutes (adjustable)
c. Open period .

over a specific period

manual_entries.title: Zone Temperature Setpoint Adjustments and Limits
manual_entries.summary: Defines occupied/unoccupied setpoints, anti-overlap logic, local adjustment limits, demand limit adjustments, window switch overrides, and occupancy sensor setbacks.

structured_payload.title: Zone Temperature Setpoint Adjustments and Limits
structured_payload.equipment_scope: All single-zone systems and subzones (VAV boxes, fan-powered boxes, etc.)
structured_payload.summary: Defines occupied/unoccupied setpoints, anti-overlap logic, local adjustment limits, demand limit adjustments, window switch overrides, and occupancy sensor setbacks.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone is in any operating mode.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Prevent heating setpoint > cooling setpoint - 0.5°C (1°F). Limit occupied cooling setpoint to 22-27°C (72-80°F) and heating to 18-22°C (65-72°F). For demand limit, increase cooling setpoint by 0.5/1/2°C (1/2/4°F) for levels 1/2/3; decrease heating similarly. For open window, set heating to 4°C (40°F) and cooling to 49°C (120°F). For occupancy sensor, after 5 min unpopulated, decrease heating by 0.5°C (1°F) and increase cooling by 0.5°C (1°F); restore after 1 min populated.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: The software shall prevent the following: a. The heating setpoint from exceeding the cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F) (i.e., the minimum difference between heating and cooling setpoints shall be 0.5°C [1°F]).

ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  65
Generic Thermal Zones
5.3.1.  This section applies to all single -zone systems and subzones  of air -handling systems, such as VAV boxes,
fan-powered boxes, etc.
5.3.2.  Setpoint s
5.3.2.1.  See Section 3.1.1.1  for zone temperature setpoint s.
5.3.2.2.  Each zone shall have separate occupied and unoccupied heating and cooling setpoint s.
5.3.2.3.  The active setpoint s shall be determined by the operating mode  of the Zone Group  (see Section
5.4.6 ).
a. The set points  shall be the occupied set points  during occupied mode, warm -up mode , and
cooldown mode .
b. The set points  shall be the unoccupied set points  during unoccupied mode, setback mode , and
setup mode .
5.3.2.4.  The software shall prevent  the following:
a. The heating setpoint  from exceeding the cooling setpoint  minus 0.5°C (1°F) (i.e., the minimum
difference between heating and  cooling setpoint s shall be 0.5°C [1°F]).

manual_entries.title: Zone Temperature Alarms
manual_entries.summary: Generates Level 4 or Level 3 alarms when zone temperature deviates significantly from setpoint for 10 minutes.

structured_payload.title: Zone Temperature Alarms
structured_payload.summary: Generates Level 4 or Level 3 alarms when zone temperature deviates significantly from setpoint for 10 minutes.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone temperature exceeds thresholds relative to setpoint.
structured_payload.required_behavior: If zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 min: Level 4 alarm. If 3°C (5°F) above for 10 min: Level 3 alarm. Similarly for low temperature: 2°C below heating setpoint for 10 min: Level 4; 3°C below for 10 min: Level 3. Suppress alarms after setpoint change and during warmup/cooldown modes.
structured_payload.fault_condition: Zone temp > cooling setpoint + 2°C for 10 min OR > +3°C for 10 min OR < heating setpoint - 2°C for 10 min OR < -3°C for 10 min
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

b. Low-temperature alarm
1. If the zone is 2°C (3°F) below heating setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.
2. If the zone is 3°C (5°F) below heating setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
Default tim e delay for zone temperature alarm (10 minutes) is intentionally long to minimize nuisance alarms. For
critical zones , such as IT closets, consider reducing time delay or setting delay to zero.

manual_entries.title: Zone Control Loops and Zone State Determination
manual_entries.summary: Separate PI control loops for heating and cooling, with enable/disable logic based on temperature relative to setpoint.

structured_payload.title: Zone Control Loops and Zone State Determination
structured_payload.summary: Separate PI control loops for heating and cooling, with enable/disable logic based on temperature relative to setpoint.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Enable heating loop when space temp < heating setpoint; disable when temp > setpoint and output zero for 30 sec. Enable cooling loop when space temp > cooling setpoint; disable when temp < setpoint and output zero for 30 sec. Use PI logic; proportional-only not acceptable. Zone state: heating if heating output nonzero and cooling zero; cooling if cooling output nonzero and heating zero; deadband otherwise.
configurable_values: P and I gains (adjustable by operator)
structured_payload.evidence_quote: The Heating Loop shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone heating set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone heating setpoint temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds.

chunk.cleaned_text:
68 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  a. The Heating Loop  shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone
heating set-point  temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone
heating setpoi nt temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain
active at all times if provisions are made to minimize integral windup.
b. The Cooling Loop  shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is above the current zone
cooling set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is below the current zone
cooling set-point  temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain
active at all times if provisions are made to minimize integral windup.
5.3.4.2.  The Coolin g Loop  shall maintain the space temperature at the active cooling setpoint . The output of
the loop shall be a software point ranging from 0% (no cooling) to 100% (full cooling).
5.3.4.3.

manual_entries.summary: Resets duct static pressure setpoint using trim-and-respond logic based on zone hot-duct static pressure requests.

structured_payload.title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV AHU Static Pressure Setpoint Reset
structured_payload.section_title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV Air-Handling Unit
structured_payload.summary: Resets duct static pressure setpoint using trim-and-respond logic based on zone hot-duct static pressure requests.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Use T&R logic with SP0=120 Pa (0.5 in. w.g.), SPmin=25 Pa (0.1 in.), SPmax=Max_DSP, Td=10 min, T=2 min, I=2, R=zone hot-duct static pressure reset requests, SPtrim=-12 Pa (-0.05 in.), SPres=15 Pa (+0.06 in.), SPres-max=32 Pa (+0.13 in.).
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.

manual_entries.summary: Resets heating supply air temperature setpoint using trim-and-respond logic during Occupied Mode; fixed at Max_HtgSAT during Warmup and Setback.

structured_payload.title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV AHU Supply Air Temperature Setpoint Reset
structured_payload.section_title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV Air-Handling Unit
structured_payload.summary: Resets heating supply air temperature setpoint using trim-and-respond logic during Occupied Mode; fixed at Max_HtgSAT during Warmup and Setback.
structured_payload.required_behavior: During Occupied Mode, use T&R logic with SP0=SPmax, SPmin=21°C (70°F), SPmax=Max_HtgSAT, Td=10 min, T=2 min, I=2, R=zone heating SAT requests, SPtrim=-0.2°C (-0.4°F), SPres=+0.3°C (+0.6°F), SPres-max=+0.8°C (+1.4°F). During Warmup and Setback, set setpoint to Max_HtgSAT.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: During Occupied Mode (Table 5.17.2.2). Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) between 21°C (70°F) and Max_HtgSAT.

manual_entries.title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV AHU AFDD Fault Conditions
manual_entries.summary: Three fault conditions: low duct static pressure with fan at full speed, SAT too low in full heating, and temperature rise across inactive heating coil.

structured_payload.title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV AHU AFDD Fault Conditions
structured_payload.section_title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV Air-Handling Unit
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Dual-fan dual-duct heating VAV AHU (heating duct)
structured_payload.summary: Three fault conditions: low duct static pressure with fan at full speed, SAT too low in full heating, and temperature rise across inactive heating coil.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: AHU is operating and not in mode change delay.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Evaluate FC#1: if DSP avg < DSPSP - εDSP and VFD speed ≥ 99% - εVFDSPD, report fault. FC#2: if SAT avg < SATSP - εSAT and HC ≥ 99%, report fault. FC#3: if RAT avg - SAT avg ≥ sqrt(εSAT²+εRAT²) + ΔTSF and HC = 0%, report fault. Use 30 min alarm delay and 30 min mode delay.

manual_entries.title: SZVAV AHU Supply Fan Speed and SAT Setpoint Reset
manual_entries.summary: Simultaneously resets fan speed and two SAT setpoints (SATsp for economizer/heating, SATsp-C for cooling coil) based on heating/cooling loop signals and outdoor air temperature.

structured_payload.title: SZVAV AHU Supply Fan Speed and SAT Setpoint Reset
structured_payload.summary: Simultaneously resets fan speed and two SAT setpoints (SATsp for economizer/heating, SATsp-C for cooling coil) based on heating/cooling loop signals and outdoor air temperature.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Fan speed: heating loop 100-50%: MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed; 50-0%: MinSpeed; deadband: MinSpeed; cooling loop 0-25%: MinSpeed; 25-50%: MinSpeed to MedSpeed; 50-75%: MedSpeed; 75-100%: MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. MedSpeed resets linearly with OAT between endpoints. SATsp: heating loop 100-50%: Heat_SAT; 50-0%: Heat_SAT to deadband value; deadband: deadband value; cooling loop 0-25%: deadband to Cool_SAT-1°C; 25-50%: unchanged; 50-75%: Cool_SAT-1°C; SATsp-C: deadband to Cool_SAT; 75-100%: unchanged. Deadband value = average of zone heating and cooling setpoints, limited to 21-24°C (70-75°F).
structured_payload.evidence_quote: For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.

chunk.cleaned_text:
152 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021
Figure 5.18.4.5 -1 Control diagram for SZVAV AHU.
5.18.4.6.  Figure 5.18.4.5 -2 separates Figure 5.18.4.5 -1 in two for clarity and to illustrate the relative
setpoint s. However, both fan speed and supply air temperature setpoint s are reset simultaneously
and by the same signal: the value of the Heating Loop  or Cooling Loop .
a. For a heating -loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to
MinSpeed.
b. For a heating -loop signal of 50% to 0%, fan speed setpoint  is MinSpeed.
c. In deadband, fan speed setpoint  is MinSpeed.
d. For a cooling -loop signal of 0% to 25%, fan speed is MinSpeed.
e. For a cooling -loop signal of 25% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MinSpeed to Med Speed.
f. For a cooling -loop signal of 50% to 75%, fan speed is MedSpeed.
g. For a cooling -loop signal of 75% to 100%, fan speed is reset from MedSpeed to
MaxCoolSpeed.
h. For a heating -loop signal of 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT.
i.

manual_entries.title: SZVAV AHU Supply Air Temperature Control Loop Mapping
manual_entries.summary: Maps SAT control loop output to modulate heating coil and economizer/return dampers in sequence.

structured_payload.title: SZVAV AHU Supply Air Temperature Control Loop Mapping
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Single-zone VAV AHU with economizer and heating coil
structured_payload.summary: Maps SAT control loop output to modulate heating coil and economizer/return dampers in sequence.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Control loop output is mapped: heating coil modulates from 0-100% over a portion of the output range; economizer damper modulates from MinOA-P to MaxOA-P over another portion; return damper is complementary. Provide separate gains for each section.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure 5.18.5.2.

chunk.cleaned_text:
154 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.18.5.  Supply Air Temperature Control
5.18.5.1.  There are two supply air temperature setpoint s, SATsp and SATsp -C. Each setpoint  is maintained
by a separate control loop, but both loops use the same supply air temperature sensor.
5.18.5.2.  The control loop for SATsp is enabled when the supply air fan is proven ON and disabled and set to
neutral otherwise.
a. Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped
to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure
5.18.5.2. Outdoor air damp er minimum MinOA -P and maximum MaxOA -P positions are
limited for economizer lockout and to maintain minimum outdoor airflow rate as described in
Sections 5.18.6 and 5.18.6.3 .
These sequences assume that the heat source can be modulated and thus control SAT to a setpoint  in heating.
chunk.text_excerpt:
154 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.18.5.  Supply Air Temperature Control
5.18.5.1.  There are two supply air temperature setpoint s, SATsp and SATsp -C. Each setpoint  is maintained
by a separate contro

evidence.evidence_text: Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure 5.18.5.2.

manual_entries.title: SZVAV AHU Minimum Outdoor Air Control without AFMS
manual_entries.summary: Controls minimum OA damper position based on fan speed and MinOAsp using linear interpolation.

structured_payload.title: SZVAV AHU Minimum Outdoor Air Control without AFMS
structured_payload.equipment_scope: SZVAV AHU without outdoor airflow measurement station
structured_payload.summary: Controls minimum OA damper position based on fan speed and MinOAsp using linear interpolation.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Calculate MinPos* and DesPos* as linear interpolation between MinPosMin/Max and DesPosMin/Max based on current fan speed. If MinOAsp=0, set MinOA-P=0. Else set MinOA-P proportional to MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate MinPos* as a linear interpolation between MinPosMin and MinPosMax based on the current fan speed.

chunk.cleaned_text:
At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode , the BAS shall calculate MinPos*
as a linear interpolation between MinPosMin and MinPosMax based on the current fan speed.
c. At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode , the BAS shall calculate DesPos*
as a linear interpolation between DesPosMin and DesPosMax based on the current fan sp eed.
d. If MinOAsp is zero, MinOA -P shall be zero (i.e., outdoor air damper fully closed).
e. If MinOAsp is nonzero, then the outdoor air damper minimum position MinOA -P shall be the
value between MinPos* and DesPos* that is proportional to the value of MinOAsp between
MinOA and DesOA.

manual_entries.title: SZVAV AHU AFDD Fault Conditions
manual_entries.summary: Fifteen fault conditions evaluated based on operating state, covering sensor errors, stuck valves, coil fouling, and economizer issues.

structured_payload.title: SZVAV AHU AFDD Fault Conditions
structured_payload.equipment_scope: SZVAV AHU with hydronic coils and economizer
structured_payload.summary: Fifteen fault conditions evaluated based on operating state, covering sensor errors, stuck valves, coil fouling, and economizer issues.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: AHU is operating and not in mode change delay.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Evaluate applicable fault conditions per OS (Table 5.18.13.6). Use 5-min rolling averages. Apply ModeDelay (30 min) after mode change and AlarmDelay (30 min) before reporting. Test mode sets delays to 0 for 120 min. Report as Level 3 alarm with description and possible diagnoses.
structured_payload.fault_condition: See Table 5.18.13.6 for 15 fault condition equations.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Table 5.18.13.6 SZVAV AHU Fault Conditions FC #2 Equation MAT AVG + Ɛ MAT < min[(RAT AVG - ƐRAT), (OAT AVG - ƐOAT)]

chunk.cleaned_text:
The OS of each AHU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating -coil control
valve, cooling -coil control valve, and economizer damper in accordance with Table 5.18.13.2  and
Figure 5.18.13.2 .
Table 5.18.13.2 SZVAV AHU Operating States
Operating State  Heating  Valve Position  Cooling Valve Position  Outdoor Air Damper
Position
#1: Heating  > 0 = 0 = MinOA -P
#2: Free cooling, modulating OA  = 0 = 0 MinOA -P < x < 100%
#3: Mechanical + economizer cooling  = 0 > 0 = 100%
#4: Mechanical cooling, minimum OA  = 0 > 0 = MinOA -P
#5: Unknown or dehumidification  No other OS applies
Figure 5.18.13.2 SZVAV AHU operating states.
The OS is distinct from , and should not be confused with , the zone status (cooling, heating, deadband ) or Zone
Group  mode (occupied, warmup , etc.).
OS#1 through  OS#4  (see Table 5.18.13.2)  represent normal operation during which a fault may nevertheless occur
if so determined by the fault condition tests in Section 5.18.13.6 .

manual_entries.title: SZVAV AHU Chilled-Water Reset Requests
manual_entries.summary: Sends requests to reset CHW temperature based on SAT deviation from setpoint and valve position.

structured_payload.title: SZVAV AHU Chilled-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload.summary: Sends requests to reset CHW temperature based on SAT deviation from setpoint and valve position.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Supply fan is proven ON and cooling is active.
structured_payload.required_behavior: If SAT > SATsp-C + 3°C for 2 min, send 3 requests. Else if SAT > SATsp-C + 2°C for 2 min, send 2 requests. Else if CHW valve > 95%, send 1 request until valve < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests.

requests.
c. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the CHW valve
position is less than 85 %.
d. Else if  the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests..

manual_entries.title: General Constant Speed Exhaust Fan Control
manual_entries.summary: Two options: operate with associated Zone Groups, or cycle to maintain room temperature.

structured_payload.title: General Constant Speed Exhaust Fan Control
structured_payload.summary: Two options: operate with associated Zone Groups, or cycle to maintain room temperature.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Option a: Fan operates when any associated supply fan is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Option b: Fan runs when zone temperature rises above active cooling setpoint until temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below setpoint for 2 minutes.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode.

than 10 %.

manual_entries.title: Fan Coil Unit Supply Fan Speed and SAT Control
manual_entries.summary: Resets fan speed and SAT setpoint based on zone state and loop signals.

structured_payload.title: Fan Coil Unit Supply Fan Speed and SAT Control
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Fan coil unit with heating and/or cooling coil
structured_payload.summary: Resets fan speed and SAT setpoint based on zone state and loop signals.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Heating: loop 100-50%: FANsp MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed, SATsp Heat_SAT; 50-0%: FANsp MinHeatSpeed, SATsp Heat_SAT to zone heating setpoint. Deadband: FANsp DeadbandSpeed (if zero, fan off). Cooling: loop 0-50%: FANsp MinCoolSpeed, SATsp zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT; 50-100%: FANsp MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed, SATsp Cool_SAT. Modulate coil with PID to maintain SATsp.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, FANsp is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed.

4. For a heating -loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone
heating setpoint.
5. The heating coil shall be  modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature
at SATsp .

manual_entries.title: Fan Coil Unit AFDD Fault Conditions
manual_entries.summary: Five fault conditions: too many OS changes, SAT too low in full heating, SAT too high in full cooling, temperature drop across inactive cooling coil, temperature rise across inactive heating coil.

structured_payload.equipment_scope: Fan coil unit with heating and cooling coils

structured_payload.trigger_condition: FCU is operating and not in mode change delay.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Evaluate applicable fault conditions per OS (Table 5.20.6.6). Use 5-min rolling averages. Apply ModeDelay (30 min) and AlarmDelay (30 min). Test mode sets delays to 0 for 120 min. Report as Level 3 alarm.

manual_entries.title: Fan Coil Unit Chilled-Water Reset Requests
manual_entries.summary: Sends CHW reset requests based on SAT deviation and valve position, suppressed if fan not at MaxCoolSpeed.

structured_payload.title: Fan Coil Unit Chilled-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Fan coil unit with chilled-water coil
structured_payload.summary: Sends CHW reset requests based on SAT deviation and valve position, suppressed if fan not at MaxCoolSpeed.
structured_payload.required_behavior: If SAT > setpoint + 10°F for 5 min, send 3 requests. Else if SAT > setpoint + 5°F for 5 min, send 2 requests. Else if CHW valve > 95%, send 1 request until valve < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed.

chunk.cleaned_text:
If There Is a Ch illed-Water Coil, Chilled -Water Reset Requests
a. All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxCoolSpeed.
The previous sequence is to prevent CHWST reset until fan is at full speed since chiller plant energy is much larger
than FC fa n energy.
b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests,
c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests,
d. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1  request until the CHW valve
position is less than 85%.
e. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
5.22.8.2.  If There Is a Chilled -Water Coil, Chiller Plant Requests. Send the chiller plant that serves the
system a chiller plant request as fol lows:
a. If the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is
less than 10%.
b.

manual_entries.summary: Sends HW reset requests based on SAT deviation from setpoint and valve position, suppressed if fan not at MaxHeatSpeed.

structured_payload.title: Fan Coil Unit Hot-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Fan coil unit with hot-water coil
structured_payload.summary: Sends HW reset requests based on SAT deviation from setpoint and valve position, suppressed if fan not at MaxHeatSpeed.
structured_payload.required_behavior: If SAT < setpoint - 30°F for 5 min, send 3 requests. Else if SAT < setpoint - 15°F for 5 min, send 2 requests. Else if HW valve > 95%, send 1 request until valve < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxHeatSpeed.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
5.22.8.3.  If There Is a Hot -Water Coil, Hot -Water Reset Requests
a. All requests shall be suppressed (send 0 requests) if fan is not at MaxHeatSpeed.
The previous sequence is to prevent HWST reset until fan is at full speed since heating plant energy is much larger
than FC fan energy.
b. If the supply air temperature is 17°C (30°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 3 requests.
c. Else if the supply air temperature is 8°C (15°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 2 requests.
d. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is
less than 8 5%.
e. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
